 {"id":"bd-kbfn","title":"Phase 2: Timer Gates (Witness integration)","description":"Implement timer gate evaluation in Witness patrol.

## Behavior
- Witness patrol includes `bd gate check --type=timer`
- Gate metadata: timeout, created_at stored on gate issue
- Expired gates escalate to Deacon/Overseer (not auto-close)

## Implementation
1. Add timer gate check to mol-witness-patrol.formula.toml
2. `bd gate check --type=timer` evaluates: now > created_at + timeout
3. Expired gates: escalate via `gt escalate --type gate_timeout`
4. Non-expired gates: skip (will check next patrol)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `bd gate check --type=timer` works
- [ ] Witness patrol runs timer check each cycle
- [ ] Expired gates trigger escalation
- [ ] Gate timeout configurable per-gate","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/polecats/quartz","created_at":"2026-01-02T01:55:40.113565-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-01-02T12:48:35.498928-08:00","closed_at":"2026-01-02T12:48:35.498928-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented bd gate check --type=timer with escalation support","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-kbfn","depends_on_id":"bd-mcva","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-02T01:55:53.92126-08:00","created_by":"mayor"}]}
 {"id":"bd-kblo","title":"bd prime should mention when beads is redirected","description":"## Problem

Agents running in a redirected clone don't know they're sharing beads with other clones. This can cause confusion when molecules or issues seem to 'appear' or 'disappear'.

## Proposed Solution

When `bd prime` runs and detects a redirect, include it in the output:

```
Beads: /Users/stevey/gt/beads/mayor/rig/.beads
       (redirected from crew/dave - you share issues with other clones)
```

## Why

Visibility over magic. If agents can see the redirect, they can reason about it.

## Related

- bd where command (shows this on demand)
- gt redirect following (ensures gt matches bd behavior)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-27T21:15:55.026907-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/dave","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:33:33.765635-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-27T21:33:33.765635-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-kff0","title":"Integrate detect-pollution into bd doctor","description":"## Task
Move `bd detect-pollution` → `bd doctor --check=pollution`

## Current state
- detect-pollution already shows deprecation hint pointing to doctor
- Doctor command exists with multiple checks

## Implementation

### 1. Update doctor.go
- Add pollution check to the doctor checks
- Add `--check=pollution` flag option
- Integrate detect-pollution logic

### 2. Update detect_pollution.go
- Mark as hidden (deprecated)
- Forward to doctor --check=pollution
- Keep for one release cycle

### 3. Update docs
- Remove detect-pollution from any command lists
- Update doctor docs to include pollution check

## Files to modify
- cmd/bd/doctor.go
- cmd/bd/detect_pollution.go

 {"id":"bd-kkka","title":"Dead code: fetchAndRebaseInWorktree() marked DEPRECATED but still exists","description":"attached_args: Remove dead code fetchAndRebaseInWorktree

The function fetchAndRebaseInWorktree() in internal/syncbranch/worktree.go (lines 811-830) is marked as DEPRECATED with a comment:

```go
// fetchAndRebaseInWorktree is DEPRECATED - kept for reference only.
// Use contentMergeRecovery instead to avoid tombstone resurrection.
```

Since contentMergeRecovery() is the replacement and is being used, this dead code should be removed to reduce maintenance burden.

 {"id":"bd-n97g","title":"bump-version.sh should detect pre-staged release notes","description":"When running the release workflow, I staged CHANGELOG.md and info.go changes before running bump-version.sh. The script:

1. Warned about uncommitted changes (correct)
2. Required stash/pop to proceed
3. After pop, the [Unreleased] header wasn't converted to version/date

The script could be smarter:
- Detect if staged changes are to CHANGELOG.md/info.go (expected for releases)
- Or provide a --allow-staged flag
- Or at minimum, re-apply the [Unreleased] → [X.Y.Z] transformation after detecting manual edits

Workaround: manually edit [Unreleased] to [0.39.1] - 2025-12-27 after stash pop.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-27T22:51:23.239642-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2025-12-27T23:36:47.949197-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-27T23:36:47.949197-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-ncwo","title":"Ghost resurrection: remote status:closed wins during git merge","description":"During bd sync, the 3-way git merge sometimes keeps remote's status:closed instead of local's status:tombstone. This causes ghost issues to resurrect even when tombstones exist.

Root cause: Git 3-way merge doesn't understand tombstone semantics. If base had closed, local changed to tombstone, and remote has closed, git might keep remote's version.

The early tombstone check in importer.go only prevents CREATION when tombstones exist in DB. But if applyDeletionsFromMerge hard-deletes the tombstones before import runs (because they're not in the merged result), the check doesn't help.

Potential fixes:
1. Make tombstones 'win' in the beads merge driver (internal/merge/merge.go)
2. Don't hard-delete tombstones in applyDeletionsFromMerge if they're in the DB
3. Export tombstones to a separate file that's not subject to merge

Ghost issues: bd-cb64c226.*, bd-cbed9619.*","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-16T22:01:03.56423-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}
 {"id":"bd-ndye","title":"mergeDependencies uses union instead of 3-way merge","description":"## Critical Bug

The `mergeDependencies` function in internal/merge/merge.go performs a UNION of left and right dependencies instead of a proper 3-way merge. This causes removed dependencies to be resurrected.

### Root Cause

```go
// Current code (lines 795-816):
func mergeDependencies(left, right []Dependency) []Dependency {
    // Just unions left + right
    // NEVER REMOVES anything
    // Doesn't even look at base!
}
```

And `mergeIssue` (line 579) doesn't pass `base`:
```go
result.Dependencies = mergeDependencies(left.Dependencies, right.Dependencies)
```

### Impact

If:
- Base has dependency D
- Left removes D (intentional)
- Right still has D (stale)

Current: D is in result (resurrection!)
Correct: Left removed it, D should NOT be in result

This breaks Gas Town's workflow and data integrity. Closed means closed.

### Fix

Change `mergeDependencies` to take `base` and do proper 3-way merge:
- If dep was in base and removed by left → exclude (left wins)
- If dep was in base and removed by right → exclude (right wins)
- If dep wasn't in base and added by either → include
- If dep was in base and both still have it → include

Key principle: **REMOVALS ARE AUTHORITATIVE**

### Files to Change

1. internal/merge/merge.go:
   - `mergeDependencies(left, right)` → `mergeDependencies(base, left, right)`
   - `mergeIssue` line 579: pass `base.Dependencies`

### Related

This also explains why `ProtectLocalExportIDs` in importer is defined but never used - the protection was never actually implemented.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T23:15:54.475872-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T23:21:10.709571-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T23:21:10.709571-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-nim5","title":"Detect/prevent child→parent dependency anti-pattern","description":"## Problem

When filing issues with dependencies, it's easy to fall into the "temporal trap":
- Thinking "Phase 1 comes before Phase 2"
- Writing `bd dep add phase1 phase2`
- But that means "phase1 depends on phase2" (backwards!)

A specific variant: children depending on their parent epic. This is ALWAYS wrong because:
1. Parent-child relationship already captures the dependency (parent closes when children done)
2. Child→parent dep creates a block: child can't start because parent is "open"
3. Parent can't close because children aren't done
4. Deadlock

## Solution

### 1. Prevent at creation (`bd dep add`)

When user runs `bd dep add X Y`:
- Check if X is a child of Y (X.id starts with Y.id + ".")
- If so, error: "Cannot add dependency: X is already a child of Y. Children inherit dependency on parent completion via hierarchy."

### 2. Detect in `bd doctor`

Add a check that finds all cases where:
- Issue A depends on Issue B
- A.id matches pattern B.id + ".*" (A is child of B)

Report as: "Child→parent dependency detected (anti-pattern)"
Offer repair: "Remove N backwards dependencies? [y/N]"

## Implementation

- `dep_add.go`: Add parent-child check before adding dependency
- `doctor.go`: Add backwards-dep detection to health checks

 {"id":"bd-nkqx","title":"bd swarm validate command","description":"Add bd swarm validate to check epic structure for swarming.

## Context
Before running a swarm, we need to validate the epic structure:
- Are dependencies correct (not inverted)?
- Are there orphaned issues?
- Is the DAG well-formed?

The Ready Front model (not phases!) means dependencies define execution order.
Common mistake: 'Phase 1 before Phase 2' → inverted deps.
Correct thinking: 'Phase 2 needs Phase 1' → bd dep add phase2 phase1

## Implementation
1. New command: bd swarm validate <epic-id>
2. Walk the dependency DAG from epic children
3. Detect issues:
   - Root issues with no dependents (orphans?)
   - Leaf issues depending on nothing (missing deps?)
   - Cycles (impossible)
   - Disconnected subgraphs
4. Report:
   - Ready fronts (waves of parallel work)
   - Estimated worker-sessions
   - Max parallelism
   - Warnings for potential issues

## Reference
~/gt/docs/swarm-architecture.md - 'The Ready Front Model' section

## Acceptance
- bd swarm validate <epic> runs
- Reports ready fronts accurately
- Warns about structural issues
- Returns success/failure exit code","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:11:08.788203-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2025-12-28T19:34:57.218009-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-28T19:34:57.218009-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-qe7j","title":"Code smell: Incomplete TODOs in formula/types.go","description":"internal/formula/types.go has multiple TODO items for features that are defined in the schema but not implemented:

- Line 170: Expand field not implemented in bd cook
- Line 174: ExpandVars field not implemented
- Line 179: Gate integration pending (bd-udsi gates)
- Line 186: Condition evaluation not implemented
- Line 210: Additional expansion features

- Schema defines features that aren't implemented

- Creates maintenance burden of unused code paths

 {"id":"bd-vqh9","title":"Code smell: init.go is 1928 lines - needs splitting","description":"cmd/bd/init.go is 1928 lines covering multiple init modes, contributor/team setup, hooks setup, and stealth mode.

**Problem:**
- File is too large to understand and maintain
- Multiple responsibilities within single file
- Harder to test individual components

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Split into logical files (init_setup.go, init_hooks.go, init_stealth.go, etc.)
